title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: TCGA diagnostic image database contains approximately 11,765 whole-slide images (WSI) SVS format ~9,640 patients across 32 cancer types. Images freely available NIH Genomic Data Commons (GDC). Two complementary ways find download images covered : imageTCGA Shiny app — interactive point--click interface clinical filters code generation GenomicDataCommons R package — programmatic access scripted large-scale downloads

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: imageTCGA app provides interactive interface full TCGA image catalogue. recommended starting point exploratory data selection.
code: imageTCGA::imageTCGA()

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: Filter panel — narrow images : Cancer type (project ID, e.g. TCGA-OV, TCGA-BRCA) Histological type Tumour grade stage Patient sex age range Dot plots — available gynaecological tumours (BRCA, OV, UCS, UCEC), showing image counts stratified clinical variables. Interactive maps — display geographic origin image three levels: institution (centre), US state, country. Code generation panel — satisfied filter selection, click Generate code. app outputs ready--run GenomicDataCommons query (see Option 2 ) can copy directly R session.

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: GenomicDataCommons Bioconductor package provides direct programmatic access GDC REST API.

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
code: BiocManager::install("GenomicDataCommons") library(GenomicDataCommons)

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
code: # Authenticate (only needed for controlled-access data; images are open-access) # gdc_token() # not required for diagnostic slides  # Build a query for diagnostic slides from ovarian cancer q <- files() |>     GenomicDataCommons::filter(         ~ cases.project.project_id == "TCGA-OV" &           data_type             == "Slide Image" &           experimental_strategy == "Diagnostic Slide"     )  # Preview results res <- results(q, size = 10) res[, c("file_id", "file_name", "file_size")]

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
code: # Add clinical filters: stage IV, high-grade serous q_filtered <- files() |>     GenomicDataCommons::filter(         ~ cases.project.project_id               == "TCGA-OV" &           data_type                              == "Slide Image" &           experimental_strategy                  == "Diagnostic Slide" &           cases.diagnoses.tumor_stage            == "stage iv" &           cases.diagnoses.morphology             == "8441/3"   # serous cystadenocarcinoma     )  res_filtered <- results(q_filtered, size = 100) nrow(res_filtered)

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
code: # See all filterable fields for files available_fields("files") |> grep("cases", x = _, value = TRUE) |> head(20)

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: downloaded SVS files can large (500 MB – 3 GB ). large-scale downloads, consider running HPC system using GDC Data Transfer Tool (command-line client).
code: # Download a single image file_ids <- results(q, size = 1)$file_id  gdcdata(     uuids           = file_ids,     destination_dir = "tcga_images/",     progress        = TRUE )

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: hundreds images, create manifest file GDC portal via R use CLI tool:
code: # Write a manifest manifest(q_filtered) |> write.table("gdc_manifest.txt",     sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE) # Then download with the GDC client gdc-client download -m gdc_manifest.txt -d tcga_images/

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: Clinical demographic metadata can retrieved alongside file metadata:
code: # Get associated case metadata case_ids <- res$cases |> sapply(\(x) x[[1]])  cases() |>     GenomicDataCommons::filter(~ case_id %in% case_ids) |>     select(c("case_id",              "diagnoses.tumor_stage",              "diagnoses.morphology",              "demographic.gender",              "demographic.age_at_index")) |>     results(size = length(case_ids))

title: Downloading TCGA Diagnostic Images
text: TCGA diagnostic images stored SVS (Aperio ScanScope) files, multi-resolution pyramidal TIFF format. R, raw SVS files typically opened directly — instead, pre-computed features (HoVerNet nuclei segmentation, Prov-GigaPath embeddings) accessed via imageFeatureTCGA without requiring original images. need inspect SVS files directly, use openslide-python library:
code: # Install the system library and Python bindings pip install openslide-bin openslide-python  # Inspect a slide in Python python3 -c " import openslide slide = openslide.OpenSlide('tcga_images/TCGA-XX-XXXX.svs') print('Levels:', slide.level_count) print('Dimensions:', slide.dimensions) print('MPP:', slide.properties.get('openslide.mpp-x')) "

title: Downstream Analyses
text: histopathology features extracted loaded Bioconductor objects, can integrated molecular omics data (genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics) downstream multi-modal analyses. vignette illustrates analytical patterns used imageTCGA manuscript. Full reproducible code available manuscript repository: https://github.com/billila/manuscript_imageTCGA/ image-derived features used : Slide-level Prov-GigaPath embeddings (768-dim vector per patient) Tile-level Prov-GigaPath embeddings (768-dim vector per tile) HoVerNet nuclei classification (cell type proportions per slide/tile) Spatial autocorrelation statistics imageTCGAutils

title: Downstream Analyses
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(dplyr) library(SummarizedExperiment)  # Import slide-level embeddings for a cohort of OV slides pgl <- getCatalog("provgigapath") |>   dplyr::filter(level == "slide_level", Project.ID == "TCGA-OV") |>   dplyr::slice(1:50) |>   getFileURLs() |>   ProvGigaList() |>   import()  pgl # SummarizedExperiment: 768 features × 50 slides # assay "embeddings" contains the Prov-GigaPath vectors

title: Downstream Analyses
code: emb_mat <- t(assay(pgl, "embeddings"))  # slides × 768  pca_res <- prcomp(emb_mat, scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE) summary(pca_res)$importance[, 1:10]  library(ggplot2) pca_df <- data.frame(   PC1    = pca_res$x[, 1],   PC2    = pca_res$x[, 2],   sample = rownames(pca_res$x) )  ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_bw() +   labs(title = "PCA — slide-level GigaPath embeddings (TCGA-OV)")

title: Downstream Analyses
code: library(uwot)  umap_res <- umap(   emb_mat,   n_neighbors = 15,   min_dist    = 0.1,   metric      = "cosine" )  umap_df <- data.frame(   UMAP1  = umap_res[, 1],   UMAP2  = umap_res[, 2],   sample = rownames(emb_mat) )  ggplot(umap_df, aes(UMAP1, UMAP2)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_bw() +   labs(title = "UMAP — slide-level GigaPath embeddings (TCGA-OV)")

title: Downstream Analyses
code: km <- kmeans(pca_res$x[, 1:20], centers = 4, nstart = 25) pca_df$cluster <- factor(km$cluster)  ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, colour = cluster)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   theme_bw()

title: Downstream Analyses
text: MOFA+ (Multi-Omics Factor Analysis, Argelaguet et al. 2020) identifies latent factors explain variance across multiple data modalities simultaneously. image embeddings used one modality alongside molecular omics data. See manuscript repository full MOFA+ analysis applied TCGA-OV: https://github.com/billila/manuscript_imageTCGA/
code: library(MOFA2)  # Prepare data as a named list of matrices (features × samples) # Each matrix must have the same column names (sample IDs) data_list <- list(   image = assay(pgl, "embeddings"),  # 768 × n_slides   rna   = rna_mat                    # genes × n_slides (your RNA matrix) )  mofa <- create_mofa(data_list)  model_opts <- get_default_model_options(mofa) model_opts$num_factors <- 15  train_opts <- get_default_training_options(mofa) train_opts$seed <- 42  mofa <- prepare_mofa(   mofa,   model_options   = model_opts,   training_options = train_opts )  mofa <- run_mofa(mofa)  # Variance explained per factor per modality plot_variance_explained(mofa, max_r2 = 15)

title: Downstream Analyses
text: Point pattern analysis treats nucleus locations spatial point processes tests clustering, regularity, complete spatial randomness. Applied HoVerNet nuclei coordinates, characterises spatial organisation different cell types across tissue section. Key metrics used manuscript: Ripley’s K / L function — tests clustering vs. regularity multiple spatial scales Cross-type K function — spatial interaction pairs cell types (e.g. neoplastic inflammatory) Nearest-neighbour distance distribution — distribution distances nearest nucleus different type analyses performed spatstat R package (x, y) coordinates HoVerNet SpatialExperiment objects.
code: library(spatstat) library(imageFeatureTCGA)  # Import HoVerNet data for one slide hspe <- getCatalog("hovernet") |>   dplyr::filter(format == "json") |>   head(1) |>   getFileURLs() |>   HoverNet(outClass = "SpatialExperiment") |>   import()  # Extract coordinates and cell types coords <- as.data.frame(spatialCoords(hspe)) types  <- colData(hspe)$label  # Create a marked point pattern win <- owin(   xrange = range(coords$x),   yrange = range(coords$y) ) pp <- ppp(x = coords$x, y = coords$y, marks = factor(types), window = win)  # Ripley's L for neoplastic cells L_neo <- Lest(subset(pp, marks == "neopla"), correction = "isotropic") plot(L_neo, main = "Ripley's L — neoplastic nuclei")  # Cross-type K: neoplastic vs. inflammatory K_cross <- Kcross(pp, i = "neopla", j = "inflam", correction = "isotropic") plot(K_cross, main = "Cross-type K: neoplastic vs. inflammatory")

title: Downstream Analyses
code: library(survival) library(survminer)  # Merge cluster assignment with clinical data surv_df <- data.frame(   sample  = names(km$cluster),   cluster = factor(km$cluster) ) |>   dplyr::left_join(clinical_data, by = "sample")  # your clinical data frame  fit <- survfit(Surv(os_days, os_status) ~ cluster, data = surv_df)  ggsurvplot(   fit, data = surv_df,   pval       = TRUE,   risk.table = TRUE,   palette    = "Set1",   title      = "Overall survival by image-derived cluster (TCGA-OV)" )

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: document describes reproduce HoVerNet nuclei segmentation results underlie pre-computed features available imageFeatureTCGA. complete setup instructions, scripts, environment files maintained hovernethelp repository Hervé Pagès. HoVer-Net (Graham et al., 2019) performs simultaneous nuclei detection, segmentation, classification histopathology whole-slide images (WSI). pipeline run TCGA diagnostic images outputs (JSON, GeoJSON, H5AD, thumbnails) accessible via imageFeatureTCGA.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Running HoVerNet scratch requires GPU resources: Processing time approximately 76–81 minutes per image single GPU node. 7-node mini-cluster (described ) achieves full TCGA dataset 15–25 hours. need pre-computed results, skip imageFeatureTCGA workflow vignette — GPU required access stored features.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: instructions follow hovernethelp setup guide.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: NVIDIA A100: NVIDIA L40S: Verify installation:
code: sudo apt-get install nvidia-linux-grid-535 sudo apt-get install nvidia-driver-550 nvidia-smi

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: environment.yml pins key packages: Python 3.12.3 PyTorch 2.5.1 opencv-python, scikit-image, scikit-learn, scipy, pandas openslide whole-slide image reading
code: # Install Miniconda wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh bash Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh conda init bash && source ~/.bashrc  # Clone HoVer-Net git clone https://github.com/vqdang/hover_net cd hover_net  # Create conda environment from hovernethelp wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hpages/hovernethelp/main/environment.yml conda env create -f environment.yml conda activate hovernet

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: pannuke_type model (TF2→PyTorch conversion) downloaded gdown: See hovernethelp setup file exact model ID path configuration.
code: pip install gdown gdown <model_gdrive_id> -O pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar tar -xf pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Images downloaded using GenomicDataCommons Bioconductor package. imageTCGA Shiny app generates query code interactively (see main workflow vignette).
code: # Example: download a single OV diagnostic image library(GenomicDataCommons)  q <- files() |>     filter(~ cases.project.project_id == "TCGA-OV" &                data_type == "Slide Image" &                experimental_strategy == "Diagnostic Slide") |>     results(size = 1)  gdcdata(q$file_id, destination_dir = "tcga_images/")

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Key parameters:
code: python run_infer.py \   --gpu='0' \   --nr_types=6 \   --type_info_path=type_info.json \   --batch_size=12 \   --model_mode=original \   --model_path=pannuke_type_tf2pytorch/hovernet_fast_pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar \   --nr_inference_workers=1 \   --nr_post_proc_workers=3 \   wsi \   --input_dir=tcga_images/ \   --output_dir=hovernet_output/ \   --save_thumb \   --save_mask

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: infer_batch2.sh script automates full pipeline: Reads manifest TCGA image IDs process Downloads SVS file via GenomicDataCommons (rsync retry, 10 attempts 5-minute intervals) Runs HoVerNet inference validates JSON output Pushes results central storage node (hoverboss) Updates manifest success/failure status timestamps
code: # Usage bash infer_batch2.sh manifest.tsv

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: input SVS image, HoVerNet produces: files exactly imageFeatureTCGA imports via HoverNet() |> import() — see imageFeatureTCGA package page load SpatialExperiment.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: hovernethelp minicluster setup describes 7-node architecture: Worker nodes (hovernet1–4, hovernet6–7, kakapo1): GPU nodes running inference Central node (hoverboss): 4 CPUs, 779 GB storage, collects results Workers sync completed JSON/H5AD files hoverboss via cron every 15 minutes. worker processes non-overlapping range TCGA image list. Total throughput: 100+ images/day, completing full TCGA dataset 15–25 hours.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: workflow demonstrates complete analysis pipeline histopathology image data Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA), using suite Bioconductor packages developed around imageTCGA ecosystem: TCGA image database contains ~11,765 diagnostic whole-slide images (WSI) ~9,640 patients across many cancer types. Pre-computed features available 54,253 files: 33,177 HoVerNet (nuclei segmentation/ classification) 21,076 Prov-GigaPath (slide- tile-level deep learning embeddings).

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: packages available GitHub active development submitted Bioconductor. Install follows:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  # Bioconductor dependencies BiocManager::install(c(     "SpatialExperiment", "SingleCellExperiment", "MultiAssayExperiment",     "BiocFileCache", "BiocIO", "BumpyMatrix", "GenomicDataCommons" ))  # imageTCGA ecosystem (GitHub) BiocManager::install(c(     "billila/imageTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageFeatureTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageTCGAutils",     "waldronlab/HistoImagePlot" ))

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: library(imageTCGA) library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(imageTCGAutils) library(HistoImagePlot) library(MultiAssayExperiment) library(SpatialExperiment) library(dplyr)

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageTCGA Shiny application provides interactive interface TCGA diagnostic image database. allows filtering cancer type, clinical parameters (histological type, grade, stage), patient geography, generates ready--run GenomicDataCommons download code. app displays: Dot plots image counts stratified clinical variables gynaecological tumours (BRCA, OV, UCS, UCEC). Interactive maps showing origin institution image country, US-state, centre level. code generation panel produces GenomicDataCommons query corresponding current filter selection. identified case(s) interest, copy generated code use next step.
code: # Launch the Shiny app imageTCGA::imageTCGA()

what: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageFeatureTCGA provides access catalogue 54,253 pre-computed feature files hosted Waldron Lab server. Two computational pipelines covered: HoVerNet – nuclei detection, segmentation, classification single-nucleus resolution. Output formats: JSON (coordinates + contours), GeoJSON, H5AD (features), PNG thumbnails. Prov-GigaPath – vision foundation model embeddings slide level (one vector per WSI) tile level (one vector per 256 × 256 px tile).

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: Key columns: pipeline, format, filename, tcga_barcode, Case.ID, Project.ID, level.
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(dplyr)  # Full catalogue (54,253 files) getCatalog()  # HoVerNet files only getCatalog("hovernet")  # ProvGigaPath files only getCatalog("provgigapath")

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: HoVerNet outputs imported SpatialExperiment columns nuclei spatial coordinates centroids slide pixel space.
code: hspe <- getCatalog("hovernet") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "json", "gz", sep = "."         )     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     HoverNet(outClass = "SpatialExperiment") |>     import()  hspe colData(hspe)

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: # Slide-level: one 768-dim vector per WSI getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "csv", "gz", sep = "."         ),         level == "slide_level"     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import()  # Tile-level: one vector per 256×256 px tile, with tile_x and tile_y coordinates getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "csv", "gz", sep = "."         ),         level == "tile_level"     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import()

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: # Import slide-level embeddings for multiple TCGA-GBM slides pgl <- getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(level == "slide_level", Project.ID == "TCGA-GBM") |>     dplyr::slice(1:3) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGigaList() |>     import()  pgl

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageTCGAutils provides utility functions dimensionality reduction spatial autocorrelation tile- nucleus-level data imported imageFeatureTCGA. Key capabilities include: PCA Prov-GigaPath tile embeddings Global Moran’s Geary’s C — spatial autocorrelation statistics Local Moran’s (LISA) — identify spatially clustered tiles Nucleus--tile matching — assign HoVerNet nuclei containing Prov-GigaPath tile See imageTCGAutils package page package documentation function-level usage.

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: HistoImagePlot renders side--side panels tissue thumbnail image HoVerNet nucleus segmentation coloured cell type labels. accepts HoVerNet outputs JSON H5AD formats fetches corresponding thumbnail automatically via BiocFileCache. See HistoImagePlot package page package documentation function-level usage.

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
Package: HistoImagePlot
text: GitHub: https://github.com/waldronlab/HistoImagePlot HistoImagePlot provides visualization tools histopathology research, working alongside imageFeatureTCGA import display HoVerNet nuclei segmentation results overlaid tissue thumbnail images. Supported HoVerNet input formats:
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(HistoImagePlot) library(dplyr) library(SpatialExperiment) library(ggplot2) library(cowplot)

title: Package: HistoImagePlot
text: Import HoVerNet H5AD file SpatialExperiment object. file automatically cached via BiocFileCache: corresponding tissue thumbnail also available store:
code: hov_file <- paste0(     "https://store.cancerdatasci.org/hovernet/h5ad/",     "TCGA-23-1021-01Z-00-DX1.F07C221B-D401-47A5-9519-10DE59CA1E9D.h5ad.gz" )  hn_spe <- HoverNet(hov_file, outClass = "SpatialExperiment") |>     import()  hn_spe thumb_path <- paste0(     "https://store.cancerdatasci.org/hovernet/thumb/",     "TCGA-23-1021-01Z-00-DX1.F07C221B-D401-47A5-9519-10DE59CA1E9D.png" )

title: Package: HistoImagePlot
text: plotHoverNetH5ADOverlay() renders nucleus centroids coloured cell type top tissue thumbnail:
code: plotHoverNetH5ADOverlay(hn_spe, thumb_path)

title: Package: HistoImagePlot
text: Point size legend appearance can adjusted:
code: plotHoverNetH5ADOverlay(     hn_spe,     thumb_path,     title             = "Ovarian Cancer Tissue - Cell Segmentation",     point_size        = 0.02,     legend_point_size = 3 )

title: Package: HistoImagePlot
text: Pass named character vector override default cell-type colours:
code: custom_colors <- c(     "no label"          = "#808080",     "neoplastic"        = "#E31A1C",     "inflammatory"      = "#1F78B4",     "stromal"           = "#33A02C",     "necrotic"          = "#FF7F00",     "benign epithelial" = "#6A3D9A" )  plotHoverNetH5ADOverlay(     hn_spe,     thumb_path,     color_palette = custom_colors,     title         = "Custom Color Scheme" )

title: Package: HistoImagePlot
text: H5AD files include additional per-nucleus statistics stored colData(): mean_intensity nearest_neighbor_distance. Spatial coordinates available via spatialCoords() x_centroid y_centroid.
code: h5ad_coords <- data.frame(spatialCoords(hn_spe), colData(hn_spe))  p1 <- ggplot(h5ad_coords, aes(x = x_centroid, y = y_centroid,                                color = mean_intensity)) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_viridis_c() +     coord_fixed() +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "Mean Intensity", color = "Intensity")  p2 <- ggplot(h5ad_coords, aes(x = x_centroid, y = y_centroid,                                color = nearest_neighbor_distance)) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +     coord_fixed() +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "Nearest Neighbor Distance", color = "Distance")  cowplot::plot_grid(p1, p2, ncol = 2)

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: GitHub: https://github.com/waldronlab/imageFeatureTCGA imageFeatureTCGA provides convenient access histopathology-derived features TCGA two complementary pipelines: HoVerNet — cell segmentation classification single-nucleus level ProvGigaPath — slide- tile-level deep learning embeddings data catalogue contains 54,253 pre-computed files: 33,177 HoVerNet files 21,076 ProvGigaPath files. files can imported directly Bioconductor objects without requiring local image files GPU resources.
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(SummarizedExperiment) library(dplyr)

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: Use getCatalog() download full catalogue available files: Filter pipeline: Key columns catalogue: pipeline, format, filename, tcga_barcode, Case.ID, Project.ID, level, lat, lon. HoVerNet formats: json, h5ad, thumb (PNG thumbnail) ProvGigaPath formats: csv (slide-level tile-level, distinguished level column: "slide_level" "tile_level")
code: getCatalog() # HoVerNet files (33,177) getCatalog("hovernet")  # ProvGigaPath files (21,076) getCatalog("provgigapath")

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: HoVerNet segmentation results imported SpatialExperiment SpatialFeatureExperiment. Columns represent individual nuclei. nucleus : x, y spatial coordinates (pixels relative slide) type — integer cell type code type_prob — classification probability label — cell type name (e.g. "neopla", "inflam", "connec", "necros")
code: hspe <- getCatalog("hovernet") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "json", "gz", sep = "."         )     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     HoverNet(outClass = "SpatialExperiment") |>     import()  hspe #> class: SpatialExperiment #> dim: 0 67081 #> metadata(1): type_map #> assays(1): counts #> colData names(10): cell_id x ... B sample_id #> spatialCoords names(2): x y colData(hspe)

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: row slide; embedding vector (768 dimensions, named V1…V768) summarises entire WSI. ProvGiga() single file returns tibble.
code: getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "csv", "gz", sep = "."         ),         level == "slide_level"     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import() #> # A tibble: 1 × 771 #>   slideName  tumorType fileName     V1     V2     V3  ... #>   <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 TCGA-VG-…  <NA>      TCGA-VG… -0.355 0.584 -0.402 … #> # ℹ 768 embedding columns (V1–V768) + 3 metadata columns

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: row 256 × 256 px tile; columns include tile_x, tile_y, 1,536 embedding dimensions (0…1535).
code: getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "csv", "gz", sep = "."         ),         level == "tile_level"     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import() #> # A tibble: 211 × 1,543 #>   slide_name  tile_id tile_name tile_x tile_y    `0`    `1`  ... #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 TCGA-VG-…        0 02155x_…   2155  18612  0.177  -1.42  … #> # ℹ 201 more rows; 1,536 embedding columns (named `0`–`1535`) + tile_x, tile_y

title: Package: imageFeatureTCGA
text: ProvGigaList() accepts multiple files . files level, import() returns single SummarizedExperiment; mixed levels returns named list.
code: # Three slide-level files for TCGA-GBM → single SummarizedExperiment (768 × 3) pgl <- getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(level == "slide_level", Project.ID == "TCGA-GBM") |>     dplyr::slice(1:3) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGigaList() |>     import()  pgl #> class: SummarizedExperiment #> dim: 768 3 #> metadata(5): slideName tumorType fileName patientIds sampleIds #> assays(1): embeddings #> colnames(3): TCGA-02-0001-01Z-00-DX1 TCGA-02-0001-01Z-00-DX2 TCGA-02-0001-01Z-00-DX3 # Both levels for the same slide → list with $slide_level and $tile_level pgl_mixed <- getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(         filename == paste(             "TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1",             "B39A4D64-82A1-4A04-8AB6-918F3058B83B",             "csv", "gz", sep = "."         ),         level %in% c("slide_level", "tile_level")     ) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGigaList() |>     import()  pgl_mixed$slide_level #> class: SummarizedExperiment #> dim: 768 1 #> assays(1): embeddings #> colnames(1): TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1  pgl_mixed$tile_level #> class: SummarizedExperiment #> dim: 1538 1 #> assays(1): tiles #> colnames(1): TCGA-VG-A8LO-01A-01-DX1

title: Package: imageTCGA
text: GitHub: https://github.com/billila/imageTCGA imageTCGA Shiny application interactive exploration TCGA diagnostic image database. provides clinical geographical filtering ~11,765 images ~9,640 patients, generates ready--run GenomicDataCommons R code downloading selected images.

title: Package: imageTCGA
code: library(imageTCGA) imageTCGA::imageTCGA()

title: Package: imageTCGA
text: left panel allows filtering : Project (cancer type, e.g. TCGA-OV, TCGA-BRCA) Histological type (morphology code) Tumour grade stage

title: Package: imageTCGA
text: gynaecological tumours (BRCA, OV, UCS, UCEC), dot plots show distribution images across clinical strata.

title: Package: imageTCGA
text: Images annotated contributing institution. map tab displays image origins three levels: Institution (centre name) US state Country

title: Package: imageTCGA
text: applying filters, Generate code panel produces GenomicDataCommons query reproduces current selection: Copy code R session, pass file_id column gdcdata() download SVS files.
code: # Example of generated code: library(GenomicDataCommons) files() |>     filter(         ~ cases.project.project_id == "TCGA-OV" &           data_type == "Slide Image" &           experimental_strategy == "Diagnostic Slide"     ) |>     results(size = 100)

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
text: GitHub: https://github.com/waldronlab/imageTCGAutils imageTCGAutils provides utility functions integrating analyzing multi-modal whole-slide image (WSI) data TCGA. designed work alongside imageFeatureTCGA, handles import pre-computed features HoVerNet Prov-GigaPath.
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(imageTCGAutils) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(sfdep) library(spdep) library(SpatialExperiment) library(data.table)

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
code: # Select an ovarian cancer slide as example tile_prov_url <- paste0(     "https://store.cancerdatasci.org/provgigapath/tile_level/",     "TCGA-23-1021-01Z-00-DX1.F07C221B-D401-47A5-9519-10DE59CA1E9D.csv.gz" )  example_slide <- ProvGiga(tile_prov_url) |>     import()

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
code: # Extract embedding columns (named with numbers: "0", "1", ...) embedding_cols <- grep("^[0-9]+$", names(example_slide), value = TRUE)  # Run PCA pca_res <- prcomp(example_slide[, embedding_cols], scale. = TRUE)  pca_example_slide <- bind_cols(     example_slide,     as_tibble(pca_res$x)[, 1:2] )  # PCA scatter plot ggplot(pca_example_slide, aes(PC1, PC2)) +     geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "Tile-level PCA Ovarian Cancer Embedding: Single Slide")  # Spatial layout coloured by PC1 ggplot(pca_example_slide, aes(tile_x, tile_y, color = PC1)) +     geom_point(size = 1) +     scale_color_viridis_c() +     coord_equal() +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "Tissue layout colored by PC1")

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
text: investigate spatial patterns, build k-nearest neighbour graph tile coordinates compute global local spatial autocorrelation PCA-reduced embeddings.
code: coords <- pca_example_slide[, c("tile_x", "tile_y")] nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 6)) lw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")

what: Global Moran’s I and Geary’s C
title: Package: imageTCGAutils
text: Moran’s ∈ [−1, 1]: positive values indicate spatial clustering. Geary’s C ∈ [0, 2]: values < 1 indicate positive spatial autocorrelation.
code: mi <- moran.test(pca_example_slide$PC1, lw) gc <- geary.test(pca_example_slide$PC1, lw)  mi gc

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
code: lisa <- localmoran(pca_example_slide$PC1, lw) pca_example_slide$localI      <- lisa[, "Ii"] pca_example_slide$localI_pval <- lisa[, "Pr(z != E(Ii))"]  # Moran scatterplot moran.plot(pca_example_slide$PC1, lw, labels = FALSE,            main = "Moran scatterplot of PC1")  # LISA spatial map ggplot(pca_example_slide, aes(x = tile_x, y = tile_y, color = localI)) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_viridis_c() +     coord_equal() +     theme_minimal() +     ggtitle("Local Moran's I (LISA) for PC1")

title: Package: imageTCGAutils
text: matchHoverNetToTiles() computes scaling factor HoVerNet nucleus coordinates Prov-GigaPath tile coordinates, assigns tile dominant cell type nucleus count based overlapping nuclei.
code: hov_file <- paste0(     "https://store.cancerdatasci.org/hovernet/h5ad/",     "TCGA-23-1021-01Z-00-DX1.F07C221B-D401-47A5-9519-10DE59CA1E9D.h5ad.gz" )  hn_spe <- HoverNet(hov_file, outClass = "SpatialExperiment") |>     import() # Visualise nucleus vs tile coordinates — they use different scales cell_coords <- spatialCoords(hn_spe)  plot(cell_coords[, 1], cell_coords[, 2],      pch = 16, col = "#0000FF20",      main = "HoVerNet nuclei (blue) vs GigaPath tiles (red)") points(pca_example_slide$tile_x, pca_example_slide$tile_y,        pch = 16, col = "#FF000020") pg_spe <- ProvGiga(tile_prov_url) |> import()  match_hv_pg <- matchHoverNetToTiles(hn_spe, pg_spe)  # Dominant cell type per tile ggplot(match_hv_pg$tiles_with_nuclei,        aes(tile_x, tile_y, color = dominant_cell_type)) +     geom_point(size = 2) +     coord_equal() +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "Per-tile dominant HoVerNet cell type")  # All cell types per tile (sized by count) ggplot(match_hv_pg$tiles_with_nuclei,        aes(tile_x, tile_y, color = cell_type_label, size = N)) +     geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +     coord_equal() +     theme_minimal() +     labs(title = "All HoVerNet cell types per tile")

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: Prov-GigaPath (Xu et al., 2024) whole-slide pathology foundation model trained 1.3 billion pathology image tiles Providence Health & Services. produces high-quality, general-purpose embeddings computational pathology. ran Prov-GigaPath available TCGA diagnostic WSIs extracted embeddings two granularities: resulting features accessible without GPU via imageFeatureTCGA — see main workflow vignette import examples. document describes extraction performed, users wish re-run extend pipeline.

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: Prov-GigaPath uses two-stage architecture: Tile encoder — DINOv2 ViT-g/14 model fine-tuned 1.3 B pathology tiles. 256 × 256 px tile 0.5 µm/px encoded 1,536-dimensional vector. Slide encoder — LongNet transformer aggregates tile-level tokens single slide-level representation, preserving spatial context via dilated attention. model weights distributed via Hugging Face (prov-gigapath/prov-gigapath) CC--NC-4.0 license. Access requires Hugging Face account accepting model terms.

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: Inference run inside Apptainer/Singularity container ensure reproducibility across different compute environments.

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: Note: Hugging Face token (HF_TOKEN) required download model weights must set environment variable runtime — embed container definition commit version control. Set runtime: apptainer run --env HF_TOKEN=<your_token> gigapath.sif
code: Bootstrap: docker From: mambaorg/micromamba:1.5.8  %files     environment.yaml /environment.yaml     . /app  %post     export MAMBA_ROOT_PREFIX=/opt/conda     export PATH=$MAMBA_ROOT_PREFIX/bin:$PATH      apt-get update && apt-get install -y git && apt-get clean      # Create the gigapath conda environment     micromamba create -y -n gigapath -f /environment.yaml      # Install the package and dependencies     cd /app     micromamba run -n gigapath pip install -e .     micromamba run -n gigapath pip install "timm>=1.0.3"     micromamba run -n gigapath pip install "openslide-bin"      micromamba clean --all --yes  %environment     export MAMBA_ROOT_PREFIX=/opt/conda     export PATH=$MAMBA_ROOT_PREFIX/envs/gigapath/bin:$PATH

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
code: apptainer build gigapath.sif gigapath.def

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: Tiles extracted 0.5 µm/px (20× equivalent magnification), tiled non-overlapping 256 × 256 px patches, encoded DINOv2 tile encoder. Output: one CSV per slide, rows = tiles, columns = x, y, d0…d1535.
code: import gigapath import torch from PIL import Image import pandas as pd import glob, os  # Load the tile encoder tile_encoder = gigapath.load_tile_encoder("hf_hub:prov-gigapath/prov-gigapath") tile_encoder.eval()  transform = gigapath.tile_transform(img_size=256)  def encode_tiles(svs_path, output_csv):     tiles = gigapath.tile_slide(svs_path, mpp=0.5, tile_size=256)     embeddings = []     for tile_img, coords in tiles:         t = transform(tile_img).unsqueeze(0)         with torch.no_grad():             emb = tile_encoder(t).squeeze().numpy()         embeddings.append({"x": coords[0], "y": coords[1], **{f"d{i}": v for i, v in enumerate(emb)}})     pd.DataFrame(embeddings).to_csv(output_csv, index=False)

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: slide encoder aggregates tile embeddings using LongNet attention. Output: one CSV per slide, single row 1,536 values.
code: slide_encoder = gigapath.load_slide_encoder("hf_hub:prov-gigapath/prov-gigapath") slide_encoder.eval()  def encode_slide(tile_csv, output_csv):     df = pd.read_csv(tile_csv)     coords = torch.tensor(df[["x", "y"]].values, dtype=torch.float32)     tile_embs = torch.tensor(df[[f"d{i}" for i in range(1536)]].values,                              dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(0)     with torch.no_grad():         slide_emb = slide_encoder(tile_embs, coords.unsqueeze(0)).squeeze().numpy()     pd.DataFrame([slide_emb]).to_csv(output_csv, index=False)

what: Batch Processing on a Compute Cluster
title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: large-scale processing across TCGA slides:
code: #!/bin/bash # Run on a SLURM cluster, one job per slide  apptainer run \     --nv \     --env HF_TOKEN="${HF_TOKEN}" \     --bind /data/tcga:/data/tcga \     --bind /data/output:/data/output \     gigapath.sif \     python extract_features.py \         --input  /data/tcga/${FILE_ID}.svs \         --output /data/output/${FILE_ID}_tiles.csv \         --level  tile

title: Prov-GigaPath Feature Extraction
text: GPU resources, pre-computed embeddings TCGA slides available via imageFeatureTCGA:
code: library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(dplyr)  # Tile-level embeddings getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(level == "tile_level", Project.ID == "TCGA-OV") |>     head(1) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import()  # Slide-level embeddings getCatalog("provgigapath") |>     dplyr::filter(level == "slide_level", Project.ID == "TCGA-OV") |>     head(1) |>     getFileURLs() |>     ProvGiga() |>     import()

title: imageTCGAWorkflow
text: end--end Bioconductor workflow histopathology image analysis using TCGA diagnostic whole-slide images.

title: imageTCGAWorkflow
text: TCGA image database contains ~11,765 diagnostic whole-slide images (WSI) ~9,640 patients. workflow covers: Data discovery — browse select images imageTCGA Shiny app Data access — download WSIs GDC using GenomicDataCommons Feature retrieval — import pre-computed HoVerNet Prov-GigaPath features via imageFeatureTCGA Spatial analysis — PCA, Moran’s , LISA imageTCGAutils Visualization — overlay cell segmentation tissue thumbnails HistoImagePlot Downstream analyses — multi-omics integration (MOFA+), point pattern analysis, survival

title: imageTCGAWorkflow
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c(     "billila/imageTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageFeatureTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageTCGAutils",     "waldronlab/HistoImagePlot" ))
